label: Sprint 30 — Pemberton RFQ pack + redline

events.text: We received an RFQ pack from Pemberton Engineering for a 3-year MRO supply framework. The documents are in the matter folder. Review them and give me your read — what needs pushback, what's standard, what's commercially material?
What's our exposure if their GTCs (Document 05) override the MSA on indemnities? Worst case, what are we signing up to?
Walk me through your top-3 redline targets and why each matters. Imagine I'm pitching this to the commercial sponsor in 5 minutes.
Anything to add given what they sent over after the first batch?
Mark up the Master Supply Agreement (02-master-supply-agreement.docx) with our redline based on your analysis. Save the output to the outputs/ subfolder of this matter.

playbook.rationale: Filename noun matches RFQ task noun. Single playbook in scope; canonical positive case.

skill.rationale: No commercial-legal skill names the 'RFQ pack review' task at its actual level (7-doc cross-document review is broader than any single slug like vendor-agreement-review).

delegate.rationale: RFQ docs (MSA + GTC + SLA + pricing + compliance annex) must be read together for coherence — explicit skip case per the doctrine.

redline.rationale: Turn 5 explicitly asks for redline of the MSA.

notes: Sprint 30 Test 1 verbatim. Persona is Helena Marwick, GC at Stanford Industrial Supply. Late-drop fixture (08-supplementary-tcs.pdf) is moved into the matter folder between Turn 3 and Turn 4 — the agent should re-list the directory and find it on Turn 4 without being told the filename.
